claim: Sequoia has 8 active AI-focused partners including Sonya Huang and Pat Grady
summary: Sequoia's website lists 8 partners with explicit AI/ML expertise. Sonya Huang leads AI investments, Pat Grady focuses on growth-stage AI companies.
snippet: Our AI practice is led by Sonya Huang, who joined in 2020 and has backed companies in foundation models, infrastructure, and applied AI.

claim: Sequoia's investment thesis focuses on 'crucible moments' — inflection points in technology
summary: Sequoia describes its approach as investing at 'crucible moments' where technology shifts create outsized opportunities. AI is currently their top crucible moment.
snippet: We look for crucible moments — inflections where bold founders can build category-defining companies.

claim: Sequoia led a $60M Series B in autonomous driving startup Wayve in late 2024
summary: TechCrunch reported Sequoia Capital participated as lead investor in Wayve's $60M Series B round announced Q4 2024, signaling continued interest in embodied AI.
snippet: Sequoia Capital led Wayve's $60 million Series B, betting on the London-based startup's approach to self-driving.

claim: Sequoia invested in 3 AI infrastructure companies in H1 2025
summary: VentureBeat reports Sequoia backed three AI infrastructure startups in the first half of 2025, focusing on inference optimization and GPU orchestration.
snippet: Sequoia has quietly built a portfolio of three AI infrastructure bets in 2025, all focused on making inference cheaper and faster.

claim: Founders report slow term sheet turnaround (2-3 weeks) compared to a16z
summary: Multiple Hacker News threads from 2024 cite Sequoia taking 2-3 weeks to issue term sheets versus a16z doing same-day decisions for competitive deals.
snippet: Applied to both Sequoia and a16z. a16z gave us a term sheet the same day. Sequoia took 18 days. By then we'd already signed.

claim: Reddit sentiment indicates Sequoia partners are seen as 'hands-off' post-investment
summary: Several r/startups threads describe Sequoia partners as providing strong initial support but becoming hands-off after the first board meeting cycle.
snippet: Sequoia was great during the raise but after the first board meeting, our partner basically disappeared. Had to chase them for intros.

claim: Sequoia portfolio companies collectively have 450+ public GitHub repos with 120K+ stars
summary: Analysis of GitHub organizations linked to Sequoia portfolio companies shows significant open-source presence, particularly in AI tooling and developer infrastructure.
snippet: Top repos by stars: vLLM (inference engine, 28K stars), LangChain (AI framework, 74K stars), both Sequoia-backed.

claim: AI portfolio companies show 40% higher commit frequency than non-AI Sequoia portfolio
summary: GitHub activity analysis shows Sequoia's AI-focused portfolio companies average 340 commits/month versus 240 for non-AI companies, indicating higher development velocity.
snippet: Comparing 6-month commit histories across 15 Sequoia companies: AI companies averaged 2,040 commits vs 1,440 for non-AI.

claim: Sequoia's latest fund (Fund XV) closed at $2.85B in 2024
summary: SEC filings and Crunchbase data show Sequoia's US/Europe Fund XV closed at approximately $2.85 billion, making it their largest fund to date.
snippet: Sequoia Capital U.S./Europe Fund XV, L.P. — $2,850,000,000 — Filed December 2024

claim: Sequoia has deployed $1.2B into AI companies since 2023
summary: Crunchbase data aggregation shows Sequoia has invested approximately $1.2 billion across 28 AI-focused companies since January 2023.
snippet: Total AI investment: ~$1.2B across 28 companies. Average check size: $42.8M. Median stage: Series B.

claim: Sequoia publicly stated AI will represent 60% of new investments in 2025
summary: In a January 2025 keynote, Sequoia managing partner Roelof Botha stated that AI-related investments would represent approximately 60% of new deals in 2025.
snippet: Botha told the audience that 'AI will be the dominant theme in our investment activity this year, representing roughly 60% of new commitments.'

claim: Sequoia is reportedly building an internal AI research lab for portfolio due diligence
summary: Recent news reports indicate Sequoia is assembling a small internal AI research team to automate parts of the due diligence process for potential investments.
snippet: Sources say Sequoia has hired 4 ML engineers to build internal tools that use LLMs to analyze pitch decks and market data.
